checks.a_analysis: I have compared the textual content of the JSON output with the source document. All headings, list items, and source information in the JSON exist in the original PDF. The alt text for the image is an expected AI generation providing a summary of the map's contents and legend, which aligns with accessibility requirements mentioned in the instructions.
The source document does not contain any phone numbers, email addresses, mailing addresses, or URLs. Correspondingly, no such information is present in the JSON output. There are no fabrications.

The source document is a single-page document containing a title, subtitle, map, legend, scale, and source info. The JSON output includes all these sections: the title and subtitle as headings, the map as an image, the legend as a list, and the sources as paragraphs. The map scale '0 25 50 100 Miles' is included within the map image object and described in the alt text.
There are no tables in this document. The legend is correctly represented as a list, and all ten items from the source legend are present in the output list.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All textual content from the headings, legend, and source attribution sections has been accurately captured in the JSON output. The text labels within the map graphic (county names and scale) are correctly treated as part of the image element and are summarized in the alt text.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the headings, legend list, and source paragraphs matches the source document exactly in terms of information and wording. The word count in the metadata (65) matches the count of the text elements extracted.

checks.a_analysis: I have examined both the source PDF and the converted JSON output. No content from the document text (headings, list items, or source citations) has been duplicated in the output. The legend information appears both in the AI-generated alt-text for the map and as a structured list, which is expected for accessibility and does not constitute a defect.

The heading hierarchy in the output is logical and follows a standard progression. The main title and date are assigned H2, while major sections like 'Parcel Updates:' and 'Sources:' are assigned H3. The sub-legend 'Most recent Quarter' is correctly nested as an H4. There are no skipped levels or incorrect nestings.
The source document does not contain any tables; it consists of a map, a legend, and source text. Therefore, no table structure was required to be preserved.

The reading order in the JSON output follows a logical top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow that faithfully represents the source document's layout. It starts with the title and date, moves to the main map image, followed by the legend, and concludes with the source information.

The legend area in the source document, which lists different quarters and their respective counts, has been correctly identified and converted into a structured unordered list in the JSON output. The items match the text found in the legend exactly.
There are no links present in the source document, and no links have been incorrectly injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The source document is a map-based infographic where information is organized spatially. The HTML conversion fails to reproduce this layout entirely, omitting the map which is the primary focus of the document. The visual organization is shifted from a graphical presentation to a linear text list, fundamentally changing how the document is interpreted. Additionally, the NCGICC logo is excessively enlarged and misplaced at the top of the content instead of the bottom right.
checks.b_evidence_source: A central map showing North Carolina counties colored according to update status, with a legend and logo at the bottom.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows a 'Contents' sidebar and a linear list of text. The map graphic is completely missing from the body of the document.

checks.a_analysis: There are no tables in the source PDF document; the data is presented as a map and a legend list. Therefore, this check passes.

Several meaningful images from the source PDF are missing in the HTML rendering. Most critically, the entire map of North Carolina counties is absent. Other missing meaningful elements include the compass rose (orienting the map), the scale bar (providing distance context), and the color swatches in the 'Parcel Updates' legend which are necessary to link the status categories to the (now missing) map regions.
checks.b_evidence_source: The map of NC, the compass rose (top left), the scale bar (bottom), and the colored squares (swatches) in the legend.
checks.b_evidence_output: None of these visual elements are present in the HTML screenshot. The legend appears as a simple bulleted list without the color indicators.

checks.a_analysis: The title and date appear both in the Table of Contents sidebar and as main headings in the body. According to the instructions, this repetition for accessibility/navigation is expected and not considered a defect. No other body content (paragraphs or lists) is duplicated.
